title: Using non-standard cryptographic algorithms is security-sensitive
description: Using non-standard cryptographic algorithms is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweThe use of a non-standard algorithm is dangerous because a determined attacker may be able to break the algorithm and compromise whatever data has been protected. Standard algorithms likeAES,RSA,SHA, …​ should be used instead.This rule tracks custom implementation of these types fromSystem.Security.Cryptographynamespace:AsymmetricAlgorithmAsymmetricKeyExchangeDeformatterAsymmetricKeyExchangeFormatterAsymmetricSignatureDeformatterAsymmetricSignatureFormatterDeriveBytesHashAlgorithmICryptoTransformSymmetricAlgorithmRecommended Secure Coding PracticesUse a standard algorithm instead of creating a custom one.Sensitive Code Examplepublic class CustomHash : HashA
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Custom cryptographic algorithm implementations are security-sensitive. Use standard algorithms like AES, RSA, SHA256 instead

logic.detection_conditions: Class inheriting from System.Security.Cryptography base types
logic.triggering_conditions: Class declaration extending HashAlgorithm, SymmetricAlgorithm, AsymmetricAlgorithm, DeriveBytes, or other crypto base classes
exclusion_conditions: Classes not inheriting from crypto base classes
never_trigger_on: Classes that don't inherit from cryptographic base types
Using standard algorithm factory methods (Aes.Create(), RSA.Create())
logic.reasoning: Custom cryptographic algorithm implementations are risky because they may have vulnerabilities that have been identified and fixed in standard implementations. Standard algorithms like AES, RSA, and SHA256 have been extensively analyzed and tested. The rule detects classes that inherit from System.Security.Cryptography base types which indicates custom implementation.
